title: poa phillipsiana
intro: Poa phillipsiana, also known as Phillips' blue grass or Phillips' meadow grass, is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is a perennial grass that is native to the mountainous regions of New Zealand and has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The plant is said to have antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which have been used to treat a range of health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. Poa phillipsiana can be prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, allowing for various herbal preparations to be made from its leaves and roots.
